description: Data model derived from the OpenDrawer metadata template. Each tier is an entity; each field includes its description, data type, and enumerated valid values with definitions where available. Former Tiers 5 and 6 have been combined into a single Tier 5.
note: Tiers 1-4 are required per the template README ('ONLY COMPLETE TIERS 1-4'). The former Tiers 5 and 6 (both marked NOT NEEDED) have been merged into a single Tier 5 (required: false).

tiers.name: Study Identity & Discovery
tiers.purpose: Make the study discoverable and uniquely identifiable; provide the minimum context needed to understand “what this is” and relate it to other studies/collections.

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: studyName
fields.description: Name of the study

fields.name: studyProjectIdentifier
fields.description: The Synapse Project identifier (synID) with which this Study is related.

fields.name: studyKey
fields.description: The short acronym for a project the study is associated with.

fields.name: studyDescription
fields.description: The synID of the wiki that contains the study description.Description of the study, including the types of experimental assays, model systems, types of analysis, description of cohort, associated vulnerable populations, special categories of data, rare diseases, etc. Maps to DUOplus2.
fields.example: Longitudinal observational study of aging, cognition, and cardiovascular health among U.S. adults aged 50 and older.

fields.name: studyAbstract
fields.description: The short study description/abstract used to describe the study that is visible on the Portal study card.
fields.example: A multi-site longitudinal cohort study following adults over 10 years to evaluate trajectories of healthy aging and chronic disease risk.

fields.name: studyMetadata
fields.description: The wiki from the CLASSIC Portal - backend that describes study metadata files

fields.name: grantNumber
fields.description: Grant number including activity code, institute code, and serial number (e.g. U01MH103392)

fields.name: principalInvestigator
fields.description: Name(s) of PI

fields.name: principalInvestigatorRoles
fields.description: Distinguishes original vs current vs co-PI

valid_values.definition: The principal investigator who initially established or led the study at its start.
The principal investigator currently responsible for the study’s scientific leadership.
An individual who shares principal investigator responsibilities with another PI.
A temporary or interim principal investigator responsible during a transition period.
More than one PI role applies or multiple PI roles are represented but not specified.
The PI role is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: principalInvestigatorContact
fields.description: Email of (each) PI

fields.name: institution
fields.description: Institution conducting study

fields.name: dataType
fields.description: Types of input/output data in bioinformatics pipelines

fields.name: dataSubtype
fields.description: Further qualification of dataType, which may be used to indicate the state of processing of the data, aggregation of the data, or presence of metadata.

fields.name: metadataType
fields.description: For files of dataSubtype: metadata, a description of the type of metadata in the file.

fields.name: metadataFileID
fields.description: Identifies a specific metadata artifact or file (i.e, a particular "measures.tsv" version, etc. If the file is uploaded to Synapse, this will be the Synapse file entity ID. Examples: syn99887766 (or FILE_STUDY_SCA_measures_v2)

fields.name: metadataCoverageScope
fields.description: Indicates whether the archived metadata describes all data collected in the study or only the data types made available for external access.

valid_values.definition: Metadata aims to cover all data types collected for the study, including those not available for external sharing.
Metadata includes only data types that are available for external access requests.
Metadata covers some collected data types beyond those shareable, but is not intended to be exhaustive.
Scope is not known or not specified.

fields.name: metadataCoverageNotes
fields.description: Free-text clarification of what is included/excluded in the metadata record and why (e.g., “some measures collected but not shareable due to licensing”).
fields.example: Metadata applies to the full study

fields.name: cohortID
fields.description: Unique identifier for the cohort

fields.name: cohortName
fields.description: Name of cohort

fields.name: longitudinalStudyID
fields.description: Linked longitudinal study identifier

tiers.name: Governance, Access & Provenance
tiers.purpose: Let users understand whether and how the dataset can be accessed and reused (policy + operational access steps), without needing back-and-forth.
tiers.effort: Low - Medium (often shareable, but sometimes requires coordination with governance teams. Often easy if links exist; harder if docs are scattered; generally safe to share)

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier
fields.description: Use studyID as the anchor, plus a persistent row identifier to identify a specific file/version, etc.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier  - value
fields.description: Enter a unique identifier for the selection made.

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.description: Indicates the level of entity to which a metadata value applies (e.g., individual participant or population summary).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value applies to an individual participant entity (participant-level).
The metadata value applies to a cohort or group of participants considered as a unit.

fields.name: aggregationLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether a metadata value represents individual-level data or an aggregate summary

valid_values.definition: The metadata value represents an individual-level value (not aggregated).
The metadata value represents an aggregate summary across a defined population scope.

fields.name: populationScope
fields.description: Identifies the population described by the metadata (e.g., study-wide, cohort, or study arm).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value summarizes or pertains to the full study population.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a specific study arm or group.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a defined cohort within the study.

fields.name: externallyRequestable
fields.description: Availability indicator to request the data.

valid_values.definition: The data are available for external request.
The data are not available for external request.
Only a subset of the data is available for external request.
External requestability is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: irbApprovalNumber
fields.description: The identifier assigned by the IRB for an approved or reviewed study, if applicable.

fields.name: irbStatus
fields.description: The regulatory determination of IRB oversight for the study.

valid_values.definition: Study reviewed and approved by an IRB
Study determined to be exempt from IRB review
IRB review or consent requirements waived
IRB review not required under applicable regulations
IRB determination not yet finalized
IRB status not known or not recorded

fields.name: irbInstitution
fields.description: The institution or ethics committee responsible for IRB review or determination.

fields.name: irbExemptionType
fields.description: The regulatory exemption category under which the study was determined to be exempt from IRB review.

valid_values.definition: Educational practices
Educational tests, surveys, interviews, public behavior
Benign behavioral interventions
Secondary research using identifiable data or biospecimens
Public benefit or service programs
Taste and food quality studies
Exempt under non–Common Rule framework
Exemption type not known or not recorded

fields.name: irbApprovalDescription
fields.description: Free-text description providing additional context about IRB approval, exemption, or oversight.

fields.name: irbScope
fields.description: Scope of IRB in relation to the study.

valid_values.definition: IRB oversight pertains to the original conduct of the study (initial enrollment and primary data collection).
IRB oversight pertains to secondary analysis of previously collected data (e.g., reuse of an existing dataset).
IRB oversight pertains to a specific site within a multi-site study (e.g., local IRB approvals).
IRB oversight pertains to sharing or reuse of data outside the original study context (e.g., repository deposition or downstream reuse).

fields.name: consentType
fields.description: Consent type (broad, tiered, etc.)

valid_values.definition: Consent permits future research use without restriction to specific diseases or narrowly defined purposes.
Consent permits some types of research uses while restricting others (i.e., multiple allowed-use options are specified).
Consent limits data use to a specific disease, condition, purpose, or study context.
Consent does not permit sharing beyond the original study team or stated original purpose.
Consent type is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: deidentificationMethod
fields.description: De-ID method applied

valid_values.definition: Direct identifiers were removed according to HIPAA Safe Harbor criteria.
An expert assessed and documented that re-identification risk is sufficiently low under applicable standards.
Direct identifiers were replaced with codes while a re-identification key may exist under separate control.
Identifiers were removed or transformed such that re-identification is not reasonably possible.
Data were shared as a HIPAA limited dataset (some indirect identifiers such as dates may be retained under agreement).
De-identification method is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: accessRestrictions
fields.description: Access level (ex., open, controlled, embargoed)

fields.name: dataUseConditions
fields.description: Data use conditions

fields.name: accessProcedure
fields.description: Brief description of the steps required to request and obtain access to the dataset.
fields.example: Submit data access request and institutional approval

fields.name: accessRequestURL
fields.description: URL to the access request page/portal or repository instructions for obtaining access.

fields.name: dataLocation
fields.description: Where the dataset is hosted or distributed (e.g., Synapse project, NIMH/NDAR collection, dbGaP).

valid_values.definition: Dataset is hosted and distributed via a Synapse project or file entity.
Dataset is hosted within the NIMH Data Archive (formerly NDAR) as a collection or study.
Dataset is deposited in the NIH Database of Genotypes and Phenotypes (dbGaP).
Dataset is hosted on Zenodo, typically with a DOI and public or restricted access settings.
Dataset is hosted on the Open Science Framework (OSF) platform.
Dataset is hosted in an institution-managed repository (e.g., university or hospital repository).
Dataset is hosted on a platform not listed above.
Hosting/distribution location is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: accessContact
fields.description: Point of contact (team, email alias, or role) for access questions.

fields.name: accessReviewBody
fields.description: The entity that reviews/approves access requests (e.g., DAC, IRB, sponsor).

valid_values.definition: Access requests are reviewed and approved by a designated Data Access Committee.
Access requests require review or approval by an IRB or ethics committee.
Access requests are reviewed and approved through repository governance processes (not a separate DAC).
Access requests require approval by the study’s principal investigator(s).
Access requests are reviewed by a body not listed above.
The approving/reviewing body is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: accessRequirements
fields.description: Checklist of materials required for access (e.g., DUA, IRB letter, training).

valid_values.definition: A data use agreement must be executed before access is granted.
Proof of IRB/ethics approval is required prior to access.
An IRB/ethics exemption determination is acceptable in lieu of full approval.
A documented plan for secure data handling/storage is required.
Completion of human subjects research training is required for requestors.
A specific account or repository identifier is required (e.g., Synapse account, eRA Commons).
Additional requirements apply that are not listed above.
Access requirements are not known or not recorded.

tiers.name: Cohort & Population
tiers.purpose: Describe who is in the study and how the cohort was defined/ascertained, enough for a user to judge relevance before requesting access.
tiers.effort: Medium (may require harmonizing terminology; some attributes may be sensitive or restricted → may keep optional where needed)

fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier
fields.description: Use studyID as the anchor, plus a persistent row identifier to identify a specific file/version, etc.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier  - value
fields.description: Enter a unique identifier for the selection made.

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.description: Indicates the level of entity to which a metadata value applies (e.g., individual participant or population summary).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value applies to an individual participant entity (participant-level).
The metadata value applies to a cohort or group of participants considered as a unit.

fields.name: aggregationLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether a metadata value represents individual-level data or an aggregate summary

valid_values.definition: The metadata value represents an individual-level value (not aggregated).
The metadata value represents an aggregate summary across a defined population scope.

fields.name: populationScope
fields.description: Identifies the population described by the metadata (e.g., study-wide, cohort, or study arm).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value summarizes or pertains to the full study population.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a specific study arm or group.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a defined cohort within the study.

fields.name: externalParticipantID
fields.description: Other external identifier, if no GUID exists

fields.name: GUID
fields.description: Global Unique Identifier: unique across studies and repositories. If a participant has been assigned a global identifier (e.g., NDAR GUID), include it here to support linkage across studies and repositories. Otherwise, leave this field blank.

fields.name: diseaseFocus
fields.description: Primary disease or condition focus of the study. A categorical descriptor summarizing participant health status selection and ascertainment context for a study cohort, intended to support discovery and comparison across studies.

valid_values.definition: Participants are selected to represent a general or normative population without requiring a specific disease or elevated risk status.
Participants are selected based on elevated risk for a condition (e.g., family history, exposure, prodromal symptoms) but may not meet diagnostic criteria.
Participants are selected because they have a specified disease or condition (or meet defined clinical criteria for that condition).
The study includes two or more contrasting groups (e.g., cases vs controls, condition vs normative, multiple conditions) to enable comparison.
Participants are identified through registries or administrative/health record sources where cohort definition is driven by existing records rather than direct recruitment.

fields.name: primaryHealthFocus
fields.description: Why participants were included: these categories describe participant health status at enrollment, not outcomes.

valid_values.definition: Participants were enrolled without requiring a specific diagnosis or elevated risk status at baseline.
Participants were enrolled because they met criteria for a specified disease/condition at baseline.
Participants were enrolled based on elevated risk indicators (e.g., family history, exposure, symptom threshold) without necessarily meeting diagnostic criteria.
The study enrolled two or more baseline health groups (e.g., condition and normative, or multiple conditions) for comparison.

fields.name: ascertainmentContext
fields.description: Where participants came from: the categories describe sampling context, independent of disease focus

valid_values.definition: Participants were recruited or identified from community sources outside of clinical care settings (e.g., advertisements, schools, workplaces).
Participants were recruited or identified through clinical care settings (e.g., clinics, hospitals, specialty practices).
Participants were identified using existing administrative sources or registries (e.g., EHR systems, claims data, disease registries).
Participants were recruited or identified using a combination of community, clinic, and/or administrative/registry sources.

fields.name: selectionIntensity
fields.description: How restrictive enrollment was: the categories describe selection methodology

valid_values.definition: Enrollment used minimal inclusion/exclusion criteria beyond basic eligibility (e.g., age range, consent capacity).
Enrollment applied multiple inclusion/exclusion criteria that narrow eligibility but remain relatively general.
Eligibility criteria are stringent or specialized, substantially narrowing eligible participants.

fields.name: populationType
fields.description: Broad classification of the population sampled in the study/cohort.

valid_values.definition: Participants are sampled to broadly represent a general population without specific health-status eligibility.
Participants are enrolled as generally healthy volunteers without a targeted disease or condition requirement.
Participants are enrolled as patients receiving care, typically in a clinical context.
Participants are enrolled based on clinical criteria, diagnosis, or engagement with clinical services.
Participants are enrolled based on indicators of elevated risk for a condition (e.g., family history, exposure, symptoms) without requiring diagnosis.
Participants are enrolled using criteria intended to increase the prevalence of a trait/condition of interest (e.g., high-risk or phenotype-enriched sampling).
Participants are enrolled because they provide care to an individual with a condition or functional need.
Participants are enrolled because they are family members of an index participant (e.g., relatives in family-based studies).
Participants are enrolled based on occupation or workplace affiliation.
Participants are enrolled from a defined workforce (e.g., employees in an organization or industry).
Participants are enrolled based on active-duty military status or affiliation.
Participants are enrolled based on veteran status.
Participants are enrolled through a school-based setting (e.g., K–12 schools) or education system.
Participants are enrolled based on student status (e.g., college or university samples).
Participants are recruited from community settings (outside clinics), such as advertisements, community orgs, or events.
Population classification is not captured by the listed categories.
Population classification is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: populationDescriptor
fields.description: Free-text descriptor capturing key population characteristics not covered by controlled terms (e.g., “male veterans”, “older adults with caregiver support”).

fields.name: ageGroup
fields.description: Broad age-based population grouping represented in the cohort.

valid_values.definition: Cohort includes participants primarily aged 65 years and older.
Cohort includes participants spanning multiple age groups.
Age group composition is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: sex
fields.description: The biological classification of a participant, typically assigned at birth based on physical and genetic characteristics.

valid_values.definition: Sex recorded as female.
Sex recorded as male.
Sex recorded as intersex or having variations of sex characteristics.
Sex is not known or not recorded.
Sex was not provided by the participant or was intentionally not reported.

fields.name: sexDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by sex category.

fields.name: sexEligible
fields.description: Sex eligibility criteria for cohort inclusion (if the study restricts enrollment).

valid_values.definition: Only participants recorded as female are eligible for enrollment.
Only participants recorded as male are eligible for enrollment.
Participants of any sex are eligible for enrollment.
Sex eligibility criteria are not specified in available documentation.
Sex eligibility criteria are not known or not recorded.

fields.name: gender
fields.description: The socially and culturally constructed identity that describes a person’s self-identified gender, which may or may not correspond to sex assigned at birth and may change over time.

valid_values.definition: Participant self-identifies as a woman.
Participant self-identifies as a man.
Participant self-identifies as non-binary.
Participant self-identifies with a gender identity not listed (e.g., genderqueer, agender); details may be captured elsewhere if allowed.
Participant chose not to disclose their gender identity.
Gender identity is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: genderDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified gender.
fields.example: Woman: 285 (57%); Man: 207 (41.4%); Nonbinary/another identity: 5 (1%); Prefer not to answer: 3 (0.6%)

fields.name: race
fields.description: Self-reported race/ethnicity. A socially defined classification that reflects a participant’s self-identified racial group(s), based on shared social, historical, or cultural experiences, and not biological or genetic differences.

valid_values.definition: Participant self-identifies as American Indian or Alaska Native.
Participant self-identifies as Asian.
Participant self-identifies as Black or African American.

valid_values.value: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander
valid_values.definition: Participant self-identifies as Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander.
Participant self-identifies as White.
Participant self-identifies as a race not listed.
Participant chose not to disclose their race.
Race is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: raceDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified race category or categories.
fields.example: White: 300 (60%); Black/African American: 80 (16%); Asian: 55 (11%); American Indian/Alaska Native: 10 (2%); Multiracial/Other: 45 (9%); Unknown: 10 (2%)

fields.name: ethnicity
fields.description: Self-reported race/ethnicity. A self-identified cultural classification that reflects whether a participant identifies as being of Hispanic or Latino origin, independent of race.

valid_values.definition: Participant self-identifies as Hispanic or Latino origin.
Participant self-identifies as not Hispanic or Latino origin.
Participant chose not to disclose ethnicity.

fields.name: ethnicityDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified ethnicity.

fields.name: generation
fields.description: Generation within family cohort (ex., F0=original, F1=child, F2=grandchild). Indicates the generational position of participants within a family-based cohort, relative to the originally enrolled participant(s).

valid_values.definition: Original enrolled participant(s) forming the initial cohort generation.
First generation descendants of the original participant(s).
Second generation descendants of the original participant(s).
Third or later generation descendants of the original participant(s).
Participants span multiple generational levels.
Generational position is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: relationToOriginal
fields.description: Relationship to original participant (ex., self, child, grandchild, spouse). Describes the familial relationship(s) of participants to the initially enrolled participant(s) in a family-based cohort.

valid_values.definition: The participant is the original enrolled participant (index participant).
The participant is a biological or adopted child of the original participant.
The participant is a biological or adopted grandchild of the original participant.
The participant is a biological or adoptive parent of the original participant.
The participant is a biological or adoptive sibling of the original participant.
The participant is a spouse or long-term partner of the original participant.
The participant is a family relation not otherwise specified.
Multiple relationship types are represented but not specified individually.
Relationship to the original participant is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: constructDomain
fields.description: Domain of measured construct

valid_values.definition: Constructs related to depressive symptoms, mood disturbance, or depression-related functioning.
Constructs related to cognitive processes (e.g., attention, memory, executive function).
Constructs related to neurological function and neurobehavioral symptoms (e.g., motor, sleep, pain).
Constructs related to physical health status and health-related behaviors (e.g., activity, diet, cardiometabolic health).
Constructs related to development, adaptive functioning, and daily living skills.
Constructs related to environmental exposures and contextual factors (e.g., SES, life events, seasonality).
Constructs related to administrative descriptors or study design variables (e.g., demographics, medication use).
Construct domain not captured by the listed categories.
Construct domain is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: eligibilityCriteriaSummary
fields.description: A brief summary of the major inclusion and exclusion criteria that determined who could enroll in the study/cohort.
fields.example: Participants ages 18–65; able to provide consent; English-speaking; no major neurological disorder or contraindication to study procedures.

fields.name: eligibilityCriteriaURL
fields.description: Link to the protocol, registry record, or document containing full inclusion/exclusion criteria.

fields.name: inclusionTags
fields.description: High-level inclusion characteristics used to define the enrolled population.

valid_values.definition: Inclusion required meeting specified age criteria (e.g., age range or minimum/maximum age).
Inclusion required meeting specified sex eligibility criteria (e.g., female-only or male-only enrollment).
Inclusion required a documented diagnosis or meeting diagnostic criteria for a condition.
Inclusion required meeting a specified symptom level or screening threshold.
Inclusion required elevated risk indicators such as family history, genetic risk, or relevant exposure.
Inclusion required a specified treatment status or medication use/non-use.
Inclusion required ability to communicate in a specified language or meet communication criteria.
Inclusion required residing in or being served by a defined geographic area or recruitment catchment.
Inclusion required access to or use of a device (e.g., smartphone) needed for participation.
Inclusion required capacity to consent or meeting consent/assent requirements.
Inclusion characteristics not captured by the listed categories.
Inclusion characteristics are not known or not recorded.

fields.name: exclusionTags
fields.description: High-level exclusion characteristics used to restrict enrollment.
fields.example: Inability to consent excluded, Neurological comorbidity excluded

valid_values.definition: Exclusion criteria removed participants with significant comorbid medical conditions.
Exclusion criteria removed participants with specified psychiatric comorbidities.
Exclusion criteria removed participants with specified neurological comorbidities.
Exclusion criteria removed participants with current or past substance use conditions as defined by the study.
Exclusion criteria removed participants with cognitive impairment beyond a specified threshold.
Exclusion criteria removed participants who were pregnant (as defined by the study protocol).
Exclusion criteria removed participants with sensory or motor limitations that would interfere with participation or measurement.
Exclusion criteria removed participants using medications that were contraindicated or would confound measurement.
Exclusion criteria removed participants unable to provide informed consent (or meet assent/consent requirements).
Exclusion criteria removed participants at elevated risk of noncompliance (e.g., inability to follow procedures or complete assessments).
Exclusion characteristics not captured by the listed categories.
Exclusion characteristics are not known or not recorded.

fields.name: criteriaSelectionIntensity
fields.description: Overall restrictiveness of inclusion/exclusion criteria.

tiers.name: Study Design & Time
tiers.purpose: Describe when and how often data were collected (longitudinal structure, bursts, waves, follow-up intervals, timestamp availability). This is key for interpretation and longitudinal/APC use cases.
tiers.effort: Medium (sometimes requires pulling info from protocol/ops docs; typically shareable)

fields.name: temporalCoverage
fields.description: The time period or periods during which data were collected for a study, cohort, or variable, summarized at an aggregate level.

fields.name: startYear
fields.description: Earliest calendar year of data collection

fields.name: endYear
fields.description: Latest calendar year of data collection

fields.name: years
fields.description: Explicit list of years, if non-continuous. Four-digit integers (e.g., 2019)

fields.name: seasons
fields.description: Seasons during which data were collected

valid_values.definition: Data collection occurred during the winter season.
Data collection occurred during the spring season.
Data collection occurred during the summer season.
Data collection occurred during the fall season.
Season(s) of data collection are not known or not recorded.

fields.name: temporalCoverageDescription
fields.description: Free-text clarification (optional)

fields.name: enrollmentAge
fields.description: The age or ages of a participant at the time of enrollment or at defined enrollment-related timepoints in a study, including single ages, age ranges, or multiple ages associated with longitudinal or staged enrollment.

fields.name: enrollmentTimepoint
fields.description: A named study milestone at which enrollment occurs (e.g., baseline, re-enrollment, extension phase).

fields.name: administrationContext
fields.description: The physical or situational setting in which a data collection activity or assessment is administered. Describes where data collection occurs, not how, who, or what. Intended for general or summary-level reporting, not per-participant metadata.

fields.name: collectionModality
fields.description: The primary mode by which data were collected for a variable or assessment.

valid_values.definition: Data were collected using a digital system (e.g., web/app, electronic form, device, sensor).
Data were collected on paper forms intended for later entry or scoring.
Data were collected in person without a digital capture system (e.g., clinical observation or interview scored offline).
Data were collected using a combination of modes (e.g., some paper-based and some digital).

fields.name: recruitmentWave
fields.description: Enrollment wave (baseline, follow-up, etc.). Identifies the recruitment phase or wave during which participants were enrolled in a study, reflecting distinct enrollment periods defined by study design or operational milestones.

valid_values.definition: Participants enrolled during the first recruitment phase.
Participants enrolled during the second recruitment phase.
Participants enrolled during the third recruitment phase.
Participants enrolled during the fourth or later recruitment phases.
Participants enrolled during a pilot or feasibility recruitment phase.
Participants enrolled during a follow-on or extension recruitment phase.
Participants span multiple recruitment waves.
Recruitment wave is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: studyWaves
fields.description: The recruitment or enrollment wave(s) represented in the study population.

valid_values.definition: The study population includes participants from the first recruitment/enrollment wave.
The study population includes participants from the second recruitment/enrollment wave.
The study population includes participants from the third recruitment/enrollment wave.
The study population includes participants from the fourth or later recruitment/enrollment waves.
The study population includes participants from a pilot or feasibility phase.
The study population includes participants from a follow-on or extension phase.
The study population includes participants from multiple waves.
Study wave representation is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: studyWaveID
fields.header_note:
if not provided as a row identifier

studyWaveID
fields.description: A stable identifier for a defined recruitment wave or enrollment phase within a study. Examples: WAVE:1, WAVE:2, WAVE:PILOT, WAVE:EXTENSION

fields.name: studyPeriodID
fields.header_note:
if not provided as a row identifier

studyPeriodID
fields.description: A stable identifier for a defined time period during which data collection occurred under consistent procedures or conditions. Examples: PERIOD:BASELINE, PERIOD:FOLLOWUP_6MO, PERIOD:COVID_ERA, PERIOD:2020_2021

fields.name: studyArmID
fields.header_note:
if not provided as a row identifier

studyArmID
fields.description: A stable identifier for a defined study arm, group, or condition assignment used to structure participant enrollment, intervention, or comparison. Examples: ARM:CONTROL, ARM:TREATMENT_A, ARM:HIGH_RISK, ARM:CASE, ARM:COMPARATOR

fields.name: studyComponentID
fields.header_note:
if not provided as a row identifier

studyComponentID
fields.description: A stable identifier for a defined component of a study (e.g., wave, arm, period) used to scope metadata summaries.

fields.name: studyComponentType
fields.description: The category of study subcomponent identified by studyComponentID (e.g., wave, arm, period), used to indicate what kind of study structure the component represents for scoping metadata summaries.

valid_values.definition: A study component representing a defined recruitment/enrollment wave.

tiers.name: Measures, Instruments, Derived Scores, Variables & Availability Summaries
tiers.note: Combined tier: former Tier 5 (Measures & Instruments) and Tier 6 (Derived Scores, Variables & Availability Summaries) merged into a single Tier 5. Shared anchor fields were de-duplicated; each field's original tier is recorded in 'source_tier'.
tiers.purpose: Describe what was measured (constructs, instrument identity, administration modality/technology) at a level that supports discovery and basic interpretability. Make derived outputs interpretable (what a score/variable represents, and its general derivation form), especially when item/trial-level data aren’t shared. Help users answer “is there enough data for my question?” without accessing participant-level files, counts, ranges, units, or timepoint-scoped summaries.
tiers.effort: Medium - High (depends on how well instruments are documented and whether devices/platform info is centralized) / High (often requires analyst input or codebook review, requires computing/curating summaries; may be restricted depending on policy→ may keep optional/add to study later, i.e., "enrichment".

field_dictionary.description: Broad age-based population grouping represented in the cohort.

field_dictionary.name: sex
field_dictionary.description: The biological classification of a participant, typically assigned at birth based on physical and genetic characteristics.

field_dictionary.name: sexDistribution
field_dictionary.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by sex category.

field_dictionary.name: sexEligible
field_dictionary.description: Sex eligibility criteria for cohort inclusion (if the study restricts enrollment).

field_dictionary.name: gender
field_dictionary.description: The socially and culturally constructed identity that describes a person’s self-identified gender, which may or may not correspond to sex assigned at birth and may change over time.

field_dictionary.name: genderDistribution
field_dictionary.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified gender.

field_dictionary.name: race
field_dictionary.description: Self-reported race/ethnicity. A socially defined classification that reflects a participant’s self-identified racial group(s), based on shared social, historical, or cultural experiences, and not biological or genetic differences.

field_dictionary.name: raceDistribution
field_dictionary.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified race category or categories.

field_dictionary.name: ethnicity
field_dictionary.description: Self-reported race/ethnicity. A self-identified cultural classification that reflects whether a participant identifies as being of Hispanic or Latino origin, independent of race.

field_dictionary.name: ethnicityDistribution
field_dictionary.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified ethnicity.

field_dictionary.name: collectionModality
field_dictionary.description: The primary mode by which data were collected for a variable or assessment.

field_dictionary.name: generation
field_dictionary.description: Generation within family cohort (ex., F0=original, F1=child, F2=grandchild). Indicates the generational position of participants within a family-based cohort, relative to the originally enrolled participant(s).

field_dictionary.name: relationToOriginal
field_dictionary.description: Relationship to original participant (ex., self, child, grandchild, spouse). Describes the familial relationship(s) of participants to the initially enrolled participant(s) in a family-based cohort.

field_dictionary.name: recruitmentWave
field_dictionary.description: Enrollment wave (baseline, follow-up, etc.). Identifies the recruitment phase or wave during which participants were enrolled in a study, reflecting distinct enrollment periods defined by study design or operational milestones.

field_dictionary.name: studyWaves
field_dictionary.description: The recruitment or enrollment wave(s) represented in the study population.

field_dictionary.name: studyWaveID
field_dictionary.description: A stable identifier for a defined recruitment wave or enrollment phase within a study. Examples: WAVE:1, WAVE:2, WAVE:PILOT, WAVE:EXTENSION

field_dictionary.name: studyPeriodID
field_dictionary.description: A stable identifier for a defined time period during which data collection occurred under consistent procedures or conditions. Examples: PERIOD:BASELINE, PERIOD:FOLLOWUP_6MO, PERIOD:COVID_ERA, PERIOD:2020_2021

field_dictionary.name: studyArmID
field_dictionary.description: A stable identifier for a defined study arm, group, or condition assignment used to structure participant enrollment, intervention, or comparison. Examples: ARM:CONTROL, ARM:TREATMENT_A, ARM:HIGH_RISK, ARM:CASE, ARM:COMPARATOR

field_dictionary.name: studyComponentID
field_dictionary.description: A stable identifier for a defined component of a study (e.g., wave, arm, period) used to scope metadata summaries.

field_dictionary.name: studyComponentType
field_dictionary.description: The category of study subcomponent identified by studyComponentID (e.g., wave, arm, period), used to indicate what kind of study structure the component represents for scoping metadata summaries.

field_dictionary.name: followUpYear
field_dictionary.description: Years since original cohort enrollment. Summarizes the amount of time elapsed since original cohort enrollment for which participant data are available.

field_dictionary.name: followUpYearDescription
field_dictionary.description: Free-text clarification (optional)

field_dictionary.name: deviceType
field_dictionary.description: The type of digital device used for data collection, applicable only when data are collected digitally.

field_dictionary.name: softwarePlatform
field_dictionary.description: The software environment or application used to deliver, collect, or manage digitally collected data.

field_dictionary.name: inputMethod
field_dictionary.description: The primary method by which participant input or data capture occurs during digital data collection.

field_dictionary.name: administrationMode
field_dictionary.description: The manner in which a digital assessment or data collection activity is administered to participants.

field_dictionary.name: manufacturerModel
field_dictionary.description: Device make/model

field_dictionary.name: operatingSystem
field_dictionary.description: OS and version

field_dictionary.name: appPlatform
field_dictionary.description: App or platform used

field_dictionary.name: sensors
field_dictionary.description: Sensors enabled (GPS, accelerometer, etc.)

field_dictionary.name: promptingScheduleType
field_dictionary.description: EMA schedule type

field_dictionary.name: promptFrequency
field_dictionary.description: Frequency of prompts

field_dictionary.name: responseWindowMinutes
field_dictionary.description: Response window (minutes)

field_dictionary.name: measureID
field_dictionary.description: Persistent per-measure row identifier. (ex., "MEAS_SCA_STROOP_v1")

field_dictionary.name: measuredConstruct
field_dictionary.description: A conceptual domain or construct that a study collected data on (e.g., depression, working memory, physical activity).

field_dictionary.name: constructDescription
field_dictionary.description: One-sentence description of how the construct was operationalized in the study.

field_dictionary.name: measureType
field_dictionary.description: The general type of measure used to assess a construct.

field_dictionary.name: isValidatedMeasure
field_dictionary.description: Indicates whether a measure or instrument used to assess a construct has been previously validated.

field_dictionary.name: measureDescription
field_dictionary.description: A brief description of how a measure was administered and what it was designed to assess.

field_dictionary.name: derivedScore
field_dictionary.description: A summary description of a score derived from a measure, including how it was calculated.

field_dictionary.name: derivationMethod
field_dictionary.description: A high-level description of how a score was derived from raw or intermediate data.

field_dictionary.name: scoreName
field_dictionary.description: Is the human-readable name of a score derived from a measure, as used or labeled by the study.

field_dictionary.name: scoreType
field_dictionary.description: Describes the general method or form by which a score was derived from a measure or assessment.

field_dictionary.name: instrumentID
field_dictionary.description: a stable identifier used to reference a specific instrument, measure, or assessment used in a study (e.g., a validated questionnaire, cognitive task, interview protocol, or sensor-based assessment).

field_dictionary.name: instrumentName
field_dictionary.description: Name of the instrument or scale used (e.g., PHQ-9, NIH Toolbox).

field_dictionary.name: instrumentSource
field_dictionary.description: Where the instrument originated

field_dictionary.name: instrumentCitation
field_dictionary.description: The primary citation(s) describing the instrument/task implementation used in the study (e.g., published paper, manual, DOI).

field_dictionary.name: instrumentVersion
field_dictionary.description: Version identifier for the instrument implementation used (e.g., vendor version, protocol revision, “v2”, “2016 revision”).

field_dictionary.name: stimulusResponseMode
field_dictionary.description: How participants interacted with the instrument during administration.

field_dictionary.name: administrationInstructions
field_dictionary.description: The instructions provided to participants describing exactly how to perform the instrument/task (verbatim or summarized).

field_dictionary.name: administrationProcedure
field_dictionary.description: The source of the implementation (e.g., NIH Toolbox, PEBL, PsychoPy script, custom app).

field_dictionary.name: protocolURL
field_dictionary.description: Link to the protocol, manual, script, or documentation describing the instrument implementation.

field_dictionary.name: constructDomain
field_dictionary.description: Domain of measured construct

field_dictionary.name: rawOrDerived
field_dictionary.description: Raw vs derived data flag

field_dictionary.name: preprocessingSteps
field_dictionary.description: Preprocessing/cleaning applied

field_dictionary.name: derivedVariableName
field_dictionary.description: The name of a derived variable or score as it appears in the dataset or metadata record.

field_dictionary.name: derivedVariableType
field_dictionary.description: A controlled category describing the general method or type of derivation used to compute a derived variable or score (e.g., sum score, composite score, standardized score).

field_dictionary.name: variableSummary
field_dictionary.description: Aggregate summary statistics describing the observed distribution and availability of a variable in a dataset, without exposing participant-level values.

field_dictionary.name: valueRange
field_dictionary.description: The observed minimum and maximum values of a continuous variable within the reported population scope.

field_dictionary.name: valueRangeMinValue
field_dictionary.description: The minimum observed value of a continuous variable within the specified population scope.

field_dictionary.name: valueRangeMaxValue
field_dictionary.description: The maximum observed value of a continuous variable within the specified population scope.

field_dictionary.name: unit
field_dictionary.description: The unit of measurement associated with a variable’s values or summary statistics (e.g., years, kg/m²).

field_dictionary.name: timepoint
field_dictionary.description: A label identifying the study timepoint or phase at which the variable value or summary applies (e.g., baseline, follow-up).

field_dictionary.name: rollingEnrollment
field_dictionary.description: Indicates whether participant enrollment occurs continuously over time rather than within a fixed recruitment window.

field_dictionary.name: studyStartYear
field_dictionary.description: The calendar year in which study recruitment or data collection began. Use "Unknown" if not available.

field_dictionary.name: burstDesign
field_dictionary.description: A summary description of the study’s burst-based sampling structure (bursts, timing, follow-up cadence, and recruitment waves).

field_dictionary.name: numberOfBursts
field_dictionary.description: The number of distinct burst periods of intensive measurement scheduled per participant.

field_dictionary.name: burstLength
field_dictionary.description: The planned duration of each burst period of intensive measurement.

field_dictionary.name: burstFrequency
field_dictionary.description: The planned frequency at which bursts occur across the study timeline.

field_dictionary.name: followUpInterval
field_dictionary.description: The planned amount of time between participant follow-up assessments, visits, or measurement periods.

field_dictionary.name: hasObservationTimestamp
field_dictionary.description: Indicates whether individual observations/records include a date or timestamp of data collection.

field_dictionary.name: eligibilityCriteriaSummary
field_dictionary.description: A brief summary of the major inclusion and exclusion criteria that determined who could enroll in the study/cohort.

field_dictionary.name: eligibilityCriteriaURL
field_dictionary.description: Link to the protocol, registry record, or document containing full inclusion/exclusion criteria.

field_dictionary.name: inclusionTags
field_dictionary.description: High-level inclusion characteristics used to define the enrolled population.

field_dictionary.name: exclusionTags
field_dictionary.description: High-level exclusion characteristics used to restrict enrollment.

field_dictionary.name: variableName
field_dictionary.description: The unique, machine-readable name assigned to a variable within a dataset.

field_dictionary.name: variableLabel
field_dictionary.description: A human-readable description of the variable that clearly explains its meaning.

field_dictionary.name: variableLevel
field_dictionary.description: Indicates the structural level of the variable within an assessment or measurement framework.

field_dictionary.name: itemText
field_dictionary.description: The exact wording of a survey or instrument item/question corresponding to the variable.

field_dictionary.name: responseType
field_dictionary.description: The format or structure of the response options for a variable.

field_dictionary.name: reverseScored
field_dictionary.description: Indicates whether the variable requires reverse scoring prior to computing subscale or total scores.

field_dictionary.name: subScale
field_dictionary.description: The named conceptual domain within an instrument to which a variable (item or score) belongs.

field_dictionary.name: recallPeriod
field_dictionary.description: The time frame respondents were instructed to consider when answering the item.

field_dictionary.name: instrumentAbbreviation
field_dictionary.description: The standardized short-form identifier for the measurement instrument from which a variable originates. This field determines the allowable values for the associated subscale attribute.

administrationContext.definition: A controlled research environment specifically designed for standardized data collection or experimentation.
A healthcare setting where data are collected as part of clinical care or clinical research activities.
A real-world setting outside controlled laboratory or clinical environments (e.g., community, workplace, school).
A participant’s personal residence where data collection occurs.
A setting in which data collection occurs without in-person contact, mediated by digital or communication technologies.
Data collection occurs across multiple administration contexts.
The administration context is not known or not recorded.

irbStatus.definition: Study reviewed and approved by an IRB
Study determined to be exempt from IRB review
IRB review or consent requirements waived
IRB review not required under applicable regulations
IRB status not known or not recorded

irbExemptionType.definition: Educational tests, surveys, interviews, public behavior
Secondary research using identifiable data or biospecimens
Exemption type not known or not recorded

metadataCoverageScope.definition: Metadata aims to cover all data types collected for the study, including those not available for external sharing.
Metadata includes only data types that are available for external access requests.
Metadata covers some collected data types beyond those shareable, but is not intended to be exhaustive.
Scope is not known or not specified.

principalInvestigatorRoles.definition: The principal investigator who initially established or led the study at its start.
The principal investigator currently responsible for the study’s scientific leadership.
An individual who shares principal investigator responsibilities with another PI.
A temporary or interim principal investigator responsible during a transition period.
More than one PI role applies or multiple PI roles are represented but not specified.
The PI role is not known or not recorded.

diseaseFocus.definition: Participants are selected to represent a general or normative population without requiring a specific disease or elevated risk status.
Participants are selected based on elevated risk for a condition (e.g., family history, exposure, prodromal symptoms) but may not meet diagnostic criteria.
Participants are selected because they have a specified disease or condition (or meet defined clinical criteria for that condition).
The study includes two or more contrasting groups (e.g., cases vs controls, condition vs normative, multiple conditions) to enable comparison.
Participants are identified through registries or administrative/health record sources where cohort definition is driven by existing records rather than direct recruitment.

primaryHealthFocus.definition: Participants were enrolled without requiring a specific diagnosis or elevated risk status at baseline.
Participants were enrolled because they met criteria for a specified disease/condition at baseline.
Participants were enrolled based on elevated risk indicators (e.g., family history, exposure, symptom threshold) without necessarily meeting diagnostic criteria.
The study enrolled two or more baseline health groups (e.g., condition and normative, or multiple conditions) for comparison.

ascertainmentContext.definition: Participants were recruited or identified from community sources outside of clinical care settings (e.g., advertisements, schools, workplaces).
Participants were recruited or identified through clinical care settings (e.g., clinics, hospitals, specialty practices).
Participants were identified using existing administrative sources or registries (e.g., EHR systems, claims data, disease registries).
Participants were recruited or identified using a combination of community, clinic, and/or administrative/registry sources.

selectionIntensity.definition: Enrollment used minimal inclusion/exclusion criteria beyond basic eligibility (e.g., age range, consent capacity).
Enrollment applied multiple inclusion/exclusion criteria that narrow eligibility but remain relatively general.
Enrollment applied stringent or specialized inclusion/exclusion criteria (e.g., specific phenotype thresholds, comorbidity exclusions, protocol adherence requirements).
The restrictiveness of enrollment criteria is not known or not recorded.
Eligibility criteria are broadly inclusive with minimal restrictions beyond basic requirements.
Eligibility criteria include multiple restrictions but remain relatively general and not highly specialized.
Eligibility criteria are stringent or specialized, substantially narrowing eligible participants.
Overall restrictiveness of eligibility criteria is not known or not recorded.

irbScope.definition: IRB oversight pertains to the original conduct of the study (initial enrollment and primary data collection).
IRB oversight pertains to secondary analysis of previously collected data (e.g., reuse of an existing dataset).
IRB oversight pertains to a specific site within a multi-site study (e.g., local IRB approvals).
IRB oversight pertains to sharing or reuse of data outside the original study context (e.g., repository deposition or downstream reuse).

consentType.definition: Consent permits future research use without restriction to specific diseases or narrowly defined purposes.
Consent permits some types of research uses while restricting others (i.e., multiple allowed-use options are specified).
Consent limits data use to a specific disease, condition, purpose, or study context.
Consent does not permit sharing beyond the original study team or stated original purpose.
Consent type is not known or not recorded.

deidentificationMethod.definition: Direct identifiers were removed according to HIPAA Safe Harbor criteria.
An expert assessed and documented that re-identification risk is sufficiently low under applicable standards.
Direct identifiers were replaced with codes while a re-identification key may exist under separate control.
Identifiers were removed or transformed such that re-identification is not reasonably possible.
Data were shared as a HIPAA limited dataset (some indirect identifiers such as dates may be retained under agreement).
De-identification method is not known or not recorded.

accessRestrictions.definition: Data are publicly available without registration or approval.
Access requires account registration and acceptance of basic terms, but not a formal review process.
Access requires review and approval (e.g., DAC review and/or a DUA) prior to obtaining the data.
Access is restricted until a specified date or condition is met.
Access is limited to a specific set of approved users or groups beyond standard controlled access processes.
Access restriction level is not known or not recorded.

dataUseConditions.definition: Use is permitted for any research purpose.
Use is permitted only for research on a specified disease or condition.
Use is permitted only for health-related or biomedical research purposes.
Use for population origins or ancestry research is prohibited.
Use for commercial purposes is prohibited.
Use for methods or tool development is prohibited.
Secondary use beyond the originally permitted purpose is not allowed.
Use requires additional ethics/IRB approval prior to research use.

dataLocation.definition: Dataset is hosted and distributed via a Synapse project or file entity.
Dataset is hosted within the NIMH Data Archive (formerly NDAR) as a collection or study.
Dataset is deposited in the NIH Database of Genotypes and Phenotypes (dbGaP).
Dataset is hosted on Zenodo, typically with a DOI and public or restricted access settings.
Dataset is hosted on the Open Science Framework (OSF) platform.
Dataset is hosted in an institution-managed repository (e.g., university or hospital repository).
Dataset is hosted on a platform not listed above.
Hosting/distribution location is not known or not recorded.

accessReviewBody.definition: Access requests are reviewed and approved by a designated Data Access Committee.
Access requests require review or approval by an IRB or ethics committee.
Access requests are reviewed and approved through repository governance processes (not a separate DAC).
Access requests require approval by the study’s principal investigator(s).
Access requests are reviewed by a body not listed above.
The approving/reviewing body is not known or not recorded.

accessRequirements.definition: A data use agreement must be executed before access is granted.
Proof of IRB/ethics approval is required prior to access.
An IRB/ethics exemption determination is acceptable in lieu of full approval.
A documented plan for secure data handling/storage is required.
An authorized institutional representative must sign required agreements or forms.
Completion of human subjects research training is required for requestors.
A specific account or repository identifier is required (e.g., Synapse account, eRA Commons).
Additional requirements apply that are not listed above.
Access requirements are not known or not recorded.

appliesTo.definition: The metadata value applies to an individual participant entity (participant-level).
The metadata value applies to a cohort or group of participants considered as a unit.

aggregationLevel.definition: The metadata value represents an individual-level value (not aggregated).

populationScope.definition: The metadata value summarizes or pertains to the full study population.

The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a defined cohort within the study.

Participants are enrolled as generally healthy volunteers without a targeted disease or condition requirement.
Participants are enrolled as patients receiving care, typically in a clinical context.
Participants are enrolled based on clinical criteria, diagnosis, or engagement with clinical services.

Participants are enrolled using criteria intended to increase the prevalence of a trait/condition of interest (e.g., high-risk or phenotype-enriched sampling).
Participants are enrolled because they provide care to an individual with a condition or functional need.
Participants are enrolled because they are family members of an index participant (e.g., relatives in family-based studies).
Participants are enrolled based on occupation or workplace affiliation.
Participants are enrolled from a defined workforce (e.g., employees in an organization or industry).
Participants are enrolled based on active-duty military status or affiliation.
Participants are enrolled based on veteran status.
Participants are enrolled based on student status (e.g., college or university samples).
Participants are enrolled through a school-based setting (e.g., K–12 schools) or education system.
Participants are recruited from community settings (outside clinics), such as advertisements, community orgs, or events.

Cohort includes participants primarily aged 65 years and older.
